name: Senda dos Encomendeiros from the Centro de Interpretación de Ombre — circular through the Parque Natural de las Fragas del Eume
name_original: Senda dos Encomendeiros desde el Centro de Interpretación de Ombre — circular por el Parque Natural de las Fragas del Eume
region_intro: Hiking around Cabanas offers diverse landscapes, from the dense forests and river valleys of the Parque Natural de las Fragas del Eume to coastal paths and historic sites. The region features varied terrain, including gentle riverside trails, routes leading to historic castles, and paths through natural parks. These areas provide a mix of elevations and environments suitable for different hiking preferences. Best hiking trails around Cabanas The most popular hiking route is Senda dos Encomendeiros from the Centro de Interpretación de Ombre — circular through the Parque Natural de las Fragas del Eume, a 8.8 miles (14.1 km) trail that takes 3 hours 48 minutes to complete. This moderate route winds through forests and along rivers, showcasing a wild environment. Another top favourite among local hikers is Castillo de Andrade — circular desde Cabanas, a moderate 8.0 miles (12.9 km) path. This trail includes a historic castle and offers views of the surrounding landscape. Local hikers also love the Caaveiro Tavern – Santa Cristina Bridge loop from Volta da Pena, a 4.0 miles (6.4 km) trail leading through the Parque Natural de las Fragas del Eume, often completed in about 1 hour 44 minutes. Hiking around Cabanas is defined by dense forests, river valleys, and historic sites. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging routes. The routes in Cabanas are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.3 stars from more than 400 reviews. More than 2,900 hikers have used komoot to explore Cabanas's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Cabanas?
A: There are over 10 hiking trails around Cabanas, offering a variety of experiences. These include routes through dense forests, along river valleys, and past historic sites, suitable for different ability levels.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in the Cabanas area?
A: Yes, Cabanas offers several circular hiking routes. A popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1345545586/senda-dos-encomendeiros-from-the-centro-de-interpretacion-de-ombre-circular-through-the-parque-natural-de-las-fragas-del-eume" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senda dos Encomendeiros from the Centro de Interpretación de Ombre — circular through the Parque Natural de las Fragas del Eume&lt;/a&gt;, which winds through forests and along rivers. Another great choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1346064800/castillo-de-andrade-circular-desde-cabanas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Andrade — circular desde Cabanas&lt;/a&gt;, which includes a historic castle.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on hikes in Cabanas?
A: Hiking in Cabanas offers diverse scenery, from the wild, jungle-like environment of the Parque Natural de las Fragas del Eume with its dense forests and river valleys, to coastal paths and historic landscapes featuring castles and bridges. You'll encounter a mix of natural beauty and cultural heritage.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to see on hikes in Cabanas?
A: Yes, the region around Cabanas features natural attractions including waterfalls. You can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/985047" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Belelle River Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which can be a beautiful sight to incorporate into your hiking plans.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the trails in Cabanas?
A: Many trails in natural areas like the Parque Natural de las Fragas del Eume are generally dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to check specific regulations for each route or protected area. Keep your dog on a leash and ensure you clean up after them.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Cabanas?
A: Yes, Cabanas offers several easy routes suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20494548" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Caaveiro Tavern – Santa Cristina Bridge loop from Volta da Pena&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4-mile trail through the Parque Natural de las Fragas del Eume, often completed in under two hours, making it a good choice for families.

Q: What historical sites or landmarks can I explore while hiking?
A: The Cabanas area is rich in history. You can explore the partially restored &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/936577" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monastery of Saint John of Caaveiro&lt;/a&gt; within the Parque Natural de las Fragas del Eume, or visit the historic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5770278" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pontedeume Bridge&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1346064800/castillo-de-andrade-circular-desde-cabanas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Andrade&lt;/a&gt; is also a significant landmark that can be part of your hiking experience.

Q: Are there options for parking near the hiking trails?
A: Many trailheads in the Cabanas area, especially those leading into natural parks or to popular attractions, typically offer designated parking areas. For specific routes, it's advisable to check the route details on komoot for parking information.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Cabanas?
A: The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant hiking conditions in Cabanas, with mild temperatures and lush landscapes. Summer can be warm, especially in forested areas, while winter can bring cooler, wetter weather, though many trails remain accessible.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Cabanas?
A: The hiking routes in Cabanas are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.3 stars from over 400 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, the beauty of the Parque Natural de las Fragas del Eume, and the historical points of interest along the way.

Q: Can I combine hiking with a visit to a beach in Cabanas?
A: Absolutely. Cabanas is known for its coastal elements. You can find routes that lead to or pass by beaches such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3069486" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Playa Grande de Miño&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3230850" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Alameda Beach&lt;/a&gt;, offering a chance to enjoy the serene coastal environment after your hike.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced hikers in Cabanas?
A: Yes, for experienced hikers seeking a challenge, there are more difficult routes available. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31909614" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Camino de Santiago Rest Area – Pontedeume Bridge loop from Vilar do Colo&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 15-mile trail with significant elevation changes, offering a demanding yet rewarding experience.
tips: This bridge is very close to the monastery of San Xoán de Caaveiro. It passes over the Eume River and is the starting point for numerous hiking trails. It is surrounded by a thick and beautiful forest, where it is a pleasure to walk at any time of the year.

Located in the heart of the Fragas de Eume natural park, between the banks of the Sesín and Eume rivers, this monastery stands in the middle of an oasis of vegetation. Although the name may be misleading, it is actually a set of buildings that can be visited: two churches, an archive, cells, etc.

waypoints.name: Monastery of Saint John of Caaveiro
